items.headline: City launches financial assistance program for water customers
items.summary: San José approved a pilot program to provide financial assistance to water customers, likely aimed at helping residents manage water bills during the drought or economic hardship. The program represents a direct effort to address affordability concerns for essential utilities.

items.headline: 27-unit townhome project with 5 affordable units proposed
items.summary: The Planning Commission considered a residential development featuring 27 three-story townhomes, including 5 affordable units. This mixed-income housing project represents a significant infill development in Cupertino.

items.headline: Lawsuit filed against city over SummerHill Homes development project
items.summary: A case called Mark Fantozzi v. City of Cupertino involving SummerHill Homes, LLC is in litigation. The city council discussed it in closed session, indicating an active dispute that residents may want to track.

items.headline: City approves 72-unit affordable housing project on Lytton Avenue
items.summary: Palo Alto greenlit a six-story, 100% affordable housing development with 72 units replacing a city parking lot at 450 Lytton Avenue. The project was processed through state streamlined review, meaning it faced fewer local restrictions than typical developments.

items.headline: Electrical infrastructure upgrade permitted for Porter Drive R&D building
items.summary: Palo Alto has issued a permit for significant electrical work at 3210 Porter Drive, including new transformers and a substation connection to support increased power demands from a new research and development tenant. The project reflects continued commercial activity in the area's office and tech corridor.

items.headline: New single-family home permitted on Battle Dance Drive
items.summary: A new single-family residence receives a building permit at 326 Battle Dance Drive under SB9 provisions, which allow property owners to split lots and build additional homes. This represents actual new housing stock rather than accessory units.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting federal immigration enforcement on city property
items.summary: City Council passed a resolution prohibiting use of city-owned and city-operated property for federal civil immigration enforcement actions. This limits cooperation between local police and ICE on municipal facilities and grounds.

items.headline: City declares hazardous vegetation a public nuisance
items.summary: Los Gatos adopted a resolution formally declaring overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation a public nuisance, enabling the city to order property owners to clear dangerous vegetation and take enforcement action if they don't comply. This gives the city stronger legal tools to address fire risk and maintain clear sightlines on properties throughout town.
